_note: Scenario/prompt-fixture skill (input is a messy user observation, not code). Rubric from fixtures/bug-report/expected-findings.md against report-export.md. The flagship discipline is the ANTI-GUESS rule: extract what's stated, mark what isn't `Unknown — not provided`, never fabricate a field. The signature failure is a report that INVENTS the missing fields (a specific browser/OS/version, an exact error, a confident root cause) to fill the template. The token `Unknown — not provided` directly tests the discipline; the must_not items fire on fabrication. Each must_surface is one groundable proposition (the #81/#84 compound-claim lesson). `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge; the LLM judge grades `claim` directly.

must_surface.claim: Extracts the actual behavior specifically: intermittently the button does nothing, and when it downloads the CSV has the header row but none of the report data (empty) — not just "broken".
Assigns a High severity justified by the stated impact — it blocks the monthly close — rather than a guessed severity.
Marks the unsupported fields (browser/OS/version, exact error) as Unknown / Needs info instead of inventing them.

must_not.claim: Must NOT fabricate the environment — inventing a specific browser / OS / version the input never states ("work laptop" is not a version).
Must NOT invent an exact error message or a confident root cause the input doesn't support — leave root cause blank / mark the error Unknown.
